pick.rationale: Duke's elite defense, ranked best in the nation, should contain Ohio State's offense and control the game. Even without injured starters Caleb Foster and potentially Patrick Ngongba, the Blue Devils' depth and offensive efficiency, built against a rigorous ACC schedule, provide a significant edge. Ohio State is a solid opponent, but the overall No. 1 seed has demonstrated the ability to win without key players and should advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Duke vs Ohio State Round of 32 Assessment
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated Duke's team quality, injury status, and Ohio State's profile to determine the likely outcome.
evidence: Duke is the No. 1 overall seed with the nation's top defense (1st in Torvik) and a top-5 offense. Injuries: Caleb Foster is out with a fractured foot; Patrick Ngongba is day-to-day but improving and trending toward playing. Duke won the ACC Tournament without both, showing depth. Ohio State has a top-30 offense but lacks comparable efficiency ratings to Duke. The Blue Devils' defensive prowess and high-major experience are expected to prevail.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://collegesportswire.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/college-basketball/2026/03/08/college-basketball-duke-caleb-foster-patrick-ngongba-injury/89052546007/ and extracted 1915 characters of text.

result.text: Duke Basketball: Injury update on Caleb Foster, Patrick Ngongba Skip to main content Home Football Basketball ACC Big 12 Big Ten Pac-12 SEC Newsletter USA TODAY Sports Jon Scheyer gives injury update on Caleb Foster, Patrick Ngongba JD McCarthy College Sports Wire The Duke Blue Devils finished their regular season with a 76-61 win over rival North Carolina on Saturday and refocused on the ACC Tournament. It was an impressive win, especially given starting center Patrick Ngbonga II sat out and starting guard Caleb Foster played just 11 minutes. Duke coach Jon Scheyer provided an injury update after the game, stating he does not expect either to play in the ACC Tournament. "I’d be shocked if both of them are playing next week," he said . He did not say what their status would be for the NCAA Tournament, where the Blue Devils (29-2) are expected to be a No. 1 seed. Ngbonga has not played since March 2 and is averaging 10.7 points, 6.0 rebounds, and 1.1 blocks per game. He is third on the team in scoring, second in rebounding and first in blocks. Foster started against North Carolina before suffering an apparent foot injury. He returned to the bench with a boot on his right foot. He is averaging 8.5 points, 2.8 assists and 3.6 rebounds for the Blue Devils. Duke is the No. 1 seed in the ACC Tournament, and its first game will be in the quarterfinals on Thursday in Charlotte, North Carolina. The Blue Devils are looking to finish as both the conference regular-season and tournament champions for the second time in three years. Contact/Follow @College_Wire on X and @College_Wires on Threads. Like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of college sports news, notes and opinions . toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://dukewire.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/duke/mens-basketball/2026/03/16/jon-scheyer-updates-status-caleb-foster-patrick-ngongba/89183971007/ and extracted 1902 characters of text.

result.text: Jon Scheyer updates status of Caleb Foster, Patrick Ngongba Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Jon Scheyer updates status of Caleb Foster, Patrick Ngongba Brian Stultz Duke Wire Duke has won 11 straight games entering the NCAA Tournament, including running through the ACC tourney for its second straight title. That run was capped by a 74-70 victory over Virginia on Saturday despite being without two starters: Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba. As the Blue Devils start the NCAA Tournament on Thursday against Siena as the top overall seed, Jon Scheyer gave an update on the status of the two players. "Caleb is going to be a little while still," Scheyer said on Sunday. "Again, he's progressing well.” Foster fractured his foot in the final regular-season game against North Carolina and has been out since. Still, his impact on the bench continues to help the Blue Devils. "Caleb, I've never seen a guy shoot so much in a scooter," Scheyer said. "Multiple times a game, I have to ask him to shut up for one second just to get in a few coaching points. While Foster is out for some time, the news seems better on Ngongba’s potential return. "As far as Pat goes, I think he's been doing his therapy, working with the medical team,” Scheyer said. “They're just benchmarks. He just has to continue to take it – it's a day-by-day thing to see how he can handle the next step and whether that soreness goes away. I can tell you he's improving. I feel very positive about it, but I can't say what that means as far as later in the week in terms of timing or readiness, because we have to take it step by step." SMS Facebook Twitter Email Help Terms of Service Subscription Terms & Conditions Privacy Policy Site Map Accessibility Our Ethical Principles Responsible Disclosure Your Privacy Choices © Copyright Gannett 2026
